texto: Outside the drizzling rain had begun again. It pattered around the house, and on the roofs and eaves, like a million, tiny, stealthy feet: softly, as though the night were teeming with a host of minute, dark beings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Evangeline Walton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Outside the drizzling rain had begun again. It pattered around the house, and on the roofs and ea…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, rain, outside pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

frase_original: Outside the drizzling rain had begun again. It pattered around the house, and on the roofs and eaves, like a million, tiny, stealthy feet: softly, as though the night were teeming with a host of minute, dark beings.

texto: The doctor from the mainland came and went. Silence settled over the island again, like a displaced curtain falling back in thickened, heavier folds. For there was a different quality in the silence now. It had tasted something, rich food on which it had long been thinly rationed. Shadowy things were trooping up, called by that scent of blood, like flies that smell carrion. They were not strangers to the old house; they had been ill-fed and at a distance, now they were hungry and avid and near.

Silence settled over the island again, like a displac…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, evil-spirits, ghost pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e ghost.

texto: Questions can be more dangerous than swords.
explicacao: Citação de Evangeline Walton sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Questions can be more dangerous than swords.” articula reflexao, danger, questions em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como questions e swords.
